user_query:
I want to institutionalize what we just learned from a debugging session and conversation. Please do the following:

1) Read these inputs carefully:
- input/transcript.md — an excerpt of our chat where I corrected some assumptions (like the package manager and correlation IDs) and asked for a new capability.
- input/errors.txt — a couple of command failures from today with relevant snippets.
- input/existing_learnings.md — a prior learning entry ID to reference if related.

2) Create structured self-improvement logs using the following output files and formats:
- output/.learnings/LEARNINGS.md — Append at least 3 new entries using the exact structured format with sections and fields (ID, Logged, Priority, Status, Area, Summary, Details, Suggested Action, Metadata). Use IDs like [LRN-YYYYMMDD-XXX], valid ISO-8601 timestamps for "Logged" (e.g., 2026-04-16T12:34:56Z), and appropriate Areas. The 3+ entries must include:
  a) A correction about our package manager (we use pnpm, not npm), linking to the prior related item with a "See Also: LRN-20250110-001" in Metadata.
  b) A best_practice about always including the X-Correlation-ID header in API responses.
  c) A knowledge_gap about needing to regenerate the TypeScript API client after OpenAPI changes (include the exact commands we use: "pnpm run generate:api" and "pnpm tsc --noEmit").
  Use clear, actionable Suggested Action sections. Where applicable, set an appropriate Priority and initial Status. At least one entry should be promoted as described below.

- output/.learnings/ERRORS.md — Append at least 2 error entries using the structured error format with sections (ID [ERR-YYYYMMDD-XXX], Logged, Priority, Status, Area, Summary, Error code block, Context, Suggested Fix, Metadata). Base these on input/errors.txt. Ensure one entry captures a "Permission denied" failure and another captures the docker build issue (include the concrete error phrase that appears in the input).

- output/.learnings/FEATURE_REQUESTS.md — Append at least 1 feature request entry using the structured format ([FEAT-YYYYMMDD-XXX], Logged, Priority, Status, Area, Requested Capability, User Context, Complexity Estimate, Suggested Implementation, Metadata). Reflect the user-requested capability found in input/transcript.md.

3) Promote two of the learnings to concise project memory files:
- Promote the pnpm vs npm learning to output/CLAUDE.md by adding a distilled bullet under a relevant section (e.g., Build & Dependencies) that explicitly mentions pnpm (and not npm). Then, in its learning entry, set Status to promoted and add a line like "Promoted: CLAUDE.md".
- Promote the API client regeneration best practice to output/AGENTS.md by adding a short actionable section (e.g., "After API Changes") that lists the exact commands: "pnpm run generate:api" and "pnpm tsc --noEmit". Then, in its learning entry, set Status to promoted and add a line like "Promoted: AGENTS.md".

Requirements and constraints:
- Use only relative workspace paths. All outputs must be written under output/ as specified. Do not use any absolute or machine-specific paths.
- Preserve the exact structured sections and bold field labels shown above. Use valid ISO-8601 timestamps with trailing Z.
- Use meaningful priorities, areas, and tags; include a Metadata block in each entry with at least Source and Tags.
- Ensure at least one new LEARNINGS.md entry includes a "See Also: LRN-20250110-001" link.
- Ensure LEARNINGS.md explicitly mentions the strings: "pnpm", "pnpm-lock.yaml", "X-Correlation-ID", "pnpm run generate:api", and "pnpm tsc --noEmit".
- Ensure ERRORS.md entries include the exact error phrases found in input/errors.txt (e.g., "Permission denied" and the docker-related phrase).

Deliverables:
- output/.learnings/LEARNINGS.md (≥3 new entries including the items above, with at least one promoted and the specified strings present)
- output/.learnings/ERRORS.md (≥2 new error entries based on the provided errors)
- output/.learnings/FEATURE_REQUESTS.md (≥1 new feature request based on the transcript)
- output/CLAUDE.md (contains a concise pnpm bullet)
- output/AGENTS.md (contains an "After API Changes" section with the exact commands)

If anything is unclear, make reasonable assumptions and document them in the Details or Metadata sections. Keep the entries specific and actionable.
